mission.title: Install and Configure Prometheus on Kubernetes
mission.description: Prometheus is a powerful monitoring system and time series database that collects metrics from configured targets, evaluates rule expressions, and triggers alerts based on specified conditions. Installing Prometheus allows you to monitor your applications and infrastructure effectively.

steps.title: Add the Prometheus Helm repository
steps.description:
First, add the Prometheus Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration:
```bash
helm repo add prometheus-community https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Prometheus using Helm
steps.description:
Now, install Prometheus in a new namespace called 'monitoring':
```bash
helm install prometheus prometheus-community/prometheus --namespace monitoring --create-namespace --version 14.0.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Prometheus pods are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace monitoring
```

steps.title: Access the Prometheus UI
steps.description:
To access the Prometheus UI, set up port forwarding:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/prometheus-server --namespace monitoring 9090:80
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
You may want to configure resource limits for the Prometheus deployment. Edit the values.yaml file or use the following command to set resource limits:
```bash
helm upgrade prometheus prometheus-community/prometheus --namespace monitoring --set server.resources.limits.cpu=500m --set server.resources.limits.memory=512Mi
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the Prometheus server running in the 'monitoring' namespace, and you will be able to access the Prometheus UI at http://localhost:9090. Ensure that the pods are in the 'Running' state.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add prometheus-community https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
helm upgrade prometheus prometheus-community/prometheus --namespace monitoring --set server.resources.limits.cpu=500m --set server.resources.limits.memory=512Mi

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and the Helm and kubectl command-line tools installed.
